title: 2016 Seattle OPA Complaint - 2016OPA-1167 - Post Mortem / Legal Analysis
content:
# 2016 Seattle False Police Report & Constitutional Violations

## Case Summary

The incident documented in Seattle OPA Case 2016OPA-1167 presents a clear pattern of constitutional violations involving police report fabrication, unlawful detention, excessive bail, and systemic oversight failures. The documentary evidence reveals that a police report fundamentally changed as it passed through three law enforcement agencies—Edmonds PD, King County Sheriff's Office, and Seattle PD—transforming from "no evidence of contact" to an allegation of direct email communication that never occurred.

## Factual Timeline & Document Analysis

### Originating Report (Edmonds PD)
Edmonds Police Officer Samuel Gagner (#2899) in Report #2015-00009660 explicitly documented: "I confirmed that there is an active order of protection in place between Chard and Nuno, however, since Nuno did not attempt to contact Chard or have another party contact her on his behalf, there was no violation of the order."

### Report Transmission & Alteration
The report was faxed from Edmonds PD to King County Sheriff's Department, then from King County Sheriff's to Seattle PD. During this inter-jurisdictional transmission, the fundamental narrative changed from documentation of no violation to an allegation that Mr Nuno sent an email to Chard in violation of the protection order.

### Seattle PD Report (GO#2016-148053)
The Seattle report claimed Chard received "an e-mail sent directly from [Mr Nuno], in violation of the Order of Protection." Critically, no email was ever: (1) captured or preserved as evidence, (2) attached to the police report, (3) investigated or verified, or (4) produced during criminal proceedings.

### Judicial Proceedings
Despite attorney Raymond Delos Reyes pointing out that no email was attached to the police report, the presiding judge stated "I don't see why a police officer would lie" and set bail at $75,000—an amount grossly excessive for alleged non-violent misdemeanor charges. Mr Nuno spent two weeks in Seattle Municipal Jail while his family raised $7,500 (10% of bail). All charges in Case #613225 were ultimately dismissed due to complete absence of evidence.

# Constitutional Violations Analysis

## Fourth Amendment: False Arrest & Unlawful Detention
- Arrest/Detention Without Probable Cause
- Continuing Unlawful Detention
- Inter-Jurisdictional Coordination Creating Liability

## Fourteenth Amendment: Due Process Violations Through Fabricated Evidence
- Deliberate Fabrication of Evidence
- Deprivation of Liberty
- Corruption of Truth-Seeking Function

## Eighth Amendment: Excessive Bail
- $75,000 Bail for Non-Violent Misdemeanors
- Based on Fabricated Evidence

## Malicious Prosecution Elements
- Initiation of Prosecution Without Probable Cause
- Favorable Termination
- Deprivation of Liberty
- Malice/Improper Purpose

## Seattle OPA Investigation Failures
- Failure to Obtain Originating Report
- Failure to Investigate Inter-Agency Transmission
- Improper Closure Without Investigation
- Policy Against Reopening

## Statute of Limitations Analysis
- Washington State: Three-year limitations period for Section 1983 claims
- Status as of November 2025: Standard SOL expired 2019-2020

## Damages Analysis
- Bail Costs: $7,500 (10% of $75,000 bail)
- Lost Wages: Two weeks detention plus ongoing case appearances
- Legal Fees: Defense costs in criminal case
- Emotional Distress and Reputational Harm

## Conclusion
This case presents egregious constitutional violations that were inadequately addressed through administrative oversight and should have been pursued through civil litigation.
